Score: 42

guidelines.rule: NEVER embed `data:<anything>;base64,...` URIs in generated mini-app HTML — image, audio, video, font, ANY type. Even one ~50KB base64 blob can exhaust the LLM's output-token budget, truncating the response mid-string and leaving an unclosed `\`\`\`miniapp` fence that the parser rejects. Replacements: (1) Audio cues → Web Audio API oscillator (4-line beep generator, ~200 bytes); (2) Icons → inline SVG strings or Unicode emoji (⏸ ▶ 🔔 ⏱); (3) Fonts → -apple-system / system-ui (already required, no @import); (4) Sample data → inline JS arrays/objects, not base64-encoded JSON.
guidelines.why: Multi-Timer 2026-05-20 reproed twice: with audio cue at zero, OpenAI agentic loop embedded data:audio/wav;base64,...50KB+... in the html JSON-stringified field, output truncated mid-base64, no closing ```miniapp fence, parser gives nil, wizard silently fails. Same exhaustion happens with data:image (already forbidden) and would happen with data:video, data:font, etc. The fix is structural: forbid ALL base64 data URIs regardless of media type.
examples: BAD: `<audio src="data:audio/wav;base64,UklGRi...(50KB)...">` — output token exhaustion
GOOD: const ctx = new AudioContext(); const osc = ctx.createOscillator(); osc.frequency.value = 880; osc.connect(ctx.destination); osc.start(); setTimeout(() => osc.stop(), 200);  // 200-byte beep

guidelines.rule: 'Offline' / 'no network' / 'no CDN' does NOT mean avoid libraries. The mChatAI platform AUTOMATICALLY vendors known web libraries to a local offline copy when you load them via a NORMAL CDN <script src> tag. Chart.js, p5.js (+p5.sound), Tone.js, pixi.js, matter.js, d3, GSAP, anime.js, tsParticles, canvas-confetti, globe.gl, and three.js are all auto-rewritten to a bundled local file at install + publish time. So loading any of these via a standard <script src='https://cdn.../the-lib.min.js'></script> IS fully offline-safe and works on the hub. ALWAYS use the REAL library; NEVER hand-roll, reimplement, stub, or write a 'lightweight compatible mini-engine' to satisfy an offline/no-CDN requirement -- that throws away the library's real capabilities and defeats the platform's offline vendoring. If the goal says 'offline' or 'no external scripts', STILL load the real library the normal way; the platform makes it offline for you. Load each library with EXACTLY ONE plain <script src='https://cdn.../lib.min.js'></script> tag in the HTML. Do NOT use a JS loader (document.createElement('script')), a try-the-CDN-then-fallback chain, an importmap, or a guessed local './libs/...' path -- the platform's offline vendoring only rewrites a literal <script src> CDN tag, so any other loading style ships an un-vendored reference that 404s offline and on the hub (observed: matter.js + canvas-confetti loaded via a fallback loader were NOT vendored).
guidelines.why: When a goal stresses 'offline / no network / no CDN', the planner and generator otherwise reimplement the library from scratch (observed: a Chart.js dashboard shipped a custom canvas charting engine instead of real Chart.js), bypassing the vendored lib + its wisdom pack and producing weaker results. The WEBLIB B-catch (bundleVendoredWebLibsIfRequested) + publish inliner already guarantee offline, so reinvention is pure waste.
examples: BAD: goal says 'must run offline' -> generator writes `class MiniChart {...}` mimicking the Chart.js API to avoid a CDN.

GOOD: `<script src='https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@4.4.3/dist/chart.umd.min.js'></script>` then `new Chart(canvas, {...})` -- the platform vendors chart.umd.min.js to ./libs/ automatically, so it runs offline with the real library.

guidelines.rule: Every button, CTA, link, and tappable control MUST do something VISIBLE when clicked/tapped -- a real state change, navigation, or action. NEVER ship a control that looks interactive but does nothing (a 'dead' decorative button is a let-down). If a control has no real backing action yet, still give it a tasteful response: clear press feedback + a short purposeful animation PLUS an inline 'Coming soon' affordance/toast, or have it (re)trigger the relevant animation/scene. Wire EVERY documented control to a handler that visibly changes the screen. A 'Replay' that restarts the animation is good; a 'Start Creating' CTA that only pulses one icon and otherwise does nothing is NOT.
guidelines.why: Lawrence 2026-06-22: a GSAP hero's 'Start Creating' button only pulsed one logo icon and did nothing else -- it reads as broken. Decorative-but-dead buttons undercut an otherwise-polished build.
examples: BAD: <button>Start Creating</button> whose click handler only animates a logo emblem and changes no state.
GOOD: the CTA opens a panel / advances a step / starts the core experience -- or, if truly not-yet-built, plays a short animation and shows an inline 'Coming soon' message.

guidelines.rule: Every function you define MUST be invoked — from the runtime loop, an event handler, or another reachable function. Before declaring the work done, trace each newly added function to its call site. A defined-but-never-called function means the feature does not exist for the user.
guidelines.why: Two consecutive incidents (Sunset Glider v2 shooter, Sling Shot Circus) shipped features as 100% dead code: fireGunner()/release()/initLevel() defined but never wired, so the app looked unchanged or broken. The harness delivery audit now hard-flags unwired functions; outputs that violate this rule get rejected and retried.
examples: pointerup handler MUST call release() if release() implements the launch
call loadProgress() then initLevel(currentLevel) during startup, not just define them
requestAnimationFrame(loop) must be started once on load for the loop to exist

guidelines.rule: When ITERATING on an existing app: preserve working systems with minimal targeted edits, and after editing verify the app still boots to its primary screen and the core interaction works end-to-end (load -> see the main scene -> perform the first action).
guidelines.why: Iteration turns that regenerate wholesale routinely break the boot path (Sling Shot Circus: only a header rendered because init was never called after a rewrite). The playability gate cannot always establish a baseline, so the generator itself must verify boot-to-interactive.
examples: after adding levels, confirm level 1 initializes on load
keep the original physics/game loop intact unless the request is explicitly about them

_changeLog.change: u-032/u-033: dead-code wiring rule + iteration boot-verification rule (Sunset Glider v2 / Sling Shot Circus incidents; pairs with the harness IT.5 delivery audit)

_changeLog: 2026-06-16: Added u-3d-goals-use-real-threejs (critical) - 3D/scene goals must render with real three.js WebGL, never a 2D canvas; offline via auto-vendored CDN importmap.

_changeLog.change: Capped examples to <=2 per guideline (was up to 10). Defense-in-depth against Claude CLI Read tool 25k-token limit; primary fix is removing the Read instruction from harness/prompts/cli-project-context.md since wisdom-universal is already injected into CLAUDE.context.md. Removed 94 surplus examples.

Phase WX-G: relabeled 12 game-themed rules from appliesTo="all"/"universal" to "anyGame". These rules are specifically about card/board game mechanics (game pieces, clip-path, chess stubs, AI opponents, scaffolds) and were inflating the systemPrompt for non-game miniApps (multi-timer, decision-maker, phonetic trainer) by ~32KB. Combined with Swift HarnessWisdomService.swift:436 (Phase WX-G code fix) that no longer treats all miniApps as games.

Phase WX-P (Phase WX-H/H2 follow-up): added u-no-base64-data-uris CRITICAL rule. Multi-Timer canary reproed (OpenAI agentic, 62KB rawResponse): LLM embeds 20KB+ base64 icons in mini-app HTML, exhausts output-token budget, response truncated mid-string, no closing ```miniapp fence, gate rejection. Forbidding the pattern in the wisdom layer is the surgical fix.

Phase WX-P2: broadened u-no-base64-data-uris to forbid ALL data:*;base64,... URIs (image, audio, video, font, anything). Initial v1 only banned data:image; LLM substituted data:audio/wav for the multi-timer audio cue and hit the same truncation. Web Audio API code replacement provided.

Phase ROUTE+CAM: added u-no-camera-microphone-getusermedia CRITICAL rule. Stop-Motion-for-kids canary called getUserMedia, crashed the app for lack of a WKUIDelegate.requestMediaCapturePermissionFor handler. Host layer now denies cleanly (won't crash) — wisdom rule pushes LLM to design around the constraint (placeholder images, emoji grid, file picker, simulated stop-motion via canvas) instead of attempting live capture.

Phase ROUTE+CAM-HOIST: moved u-no-camera-microphone-getusermedia to position #2 (right after u-001 interaction rule) so it survives the 16KB autofix truncation cap. Initial position was at the bottom of guidelines; even with severity-sort in Swift (HarnessWisdomService.swift Phase ROUTE+SEVERITY), the rule was within the critical block but at index N-1 of critical rules — still dropped by `wisdom.prefix(16KB)`. Hoisting + severity sort = double safety.
